mnemonics: BIG HAM: Remember the unique challenges of processing Indian languages: B - Bilingual, I - Indian, G - Graphemes, H - Hindi, A - Agglutinative, M - Morphology
keyPoints: Indian languages are morphologically rich and often agglutinative
They use non-Latin scripts (e.g., Devanagari, Bengali, Tamil) requiring specialized tokenization
Code-mixing (e.g., Hinglish) presents additional processing challenges
commonMistakes: Assuming English NLP techniques work directly on Indian languages without modification
Overlooking the importance of script normalization and encoding (e.g., Unicode issues)
Ignoring dialectal variations and code-switching patterns in training data
